commit.message:
🔧 Fix: Add automatic retry logic for database connections

PROBLEM:
- InterfaceError: connection already closed still occurring
- Happens when Streamlit reruns and connection is closed
- Between page transitions, connections could be terminated

SOLUTION:
- Added execute_query_safe() wrapper function
- Automatic retry (2 attempts) on connection failure
- Handles both InterfaceError and OperationalError
- Connection is opened and closed within function scope
- Guarantees that connection is fresh for each query

BENEFITS:
- Resilient to server closing old connections
- Works reliably with cloud/remote databases
- User sees data even if first attempt fails
- Automatic recovery without manual intervention

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- CSI シーケンス (\x1b[...) を終端バイトまで丸ごとスキップするよう修正

commit.message:
🔧 Fix: Remove connection caching to prevent 'already closed' errors

PROBLEM:
- @st.cache_resource was keeping database connection in cache
- Between Streamlit reruns, server would close the connection
- Next query would fail with 'InterfaceError: connection already closed'

SOLUTION:
- Removed @st.cache_resource from get_db_conn()
- Each data load now creates a fresh connection
- Lightweight and reliable - PostgreSQL handles connection pooling

BENEFITS:
- No stale connections
- Works better with cloud database (remote server can close old conns)
- Each query gets a fresh, clean connection
- Faster failure detection with connect_timeout=5

This is the correct pattern for Streamlit + PostgreSQL.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use force_update on seek to fix frontend progress sync

The MA server filters elapsed_time-only changes and sends a
lightweight QUEUE_TIME_UPDATED event instead of a full PLAYER_UPDATED.
The frontend does not always pick up these lightweight events for
PluginSource players, so the progress bar stays stale until page reload.

Pass force_update=True on significant changes (seek, track change) so
the server broadcasts a full PLAYER_UPDATED event that the frontend
handles correctly.  Regular throttled updates continue without
force_update to avoid unnecessary event bus traffic.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix SF2 pattern scroll jitter: hook into onaudioprocess for position

Root cause: setInterval(4ms) polling detected row changes at variable
offsets after the emulator advanced (0-23ms jitter per 1024-sample buffer).

Fix: wrap ScriptProcessorNode.onaudioprocess to call position check
immediately after each buffer fill — the emulator just advanced, so
readRAM returns the freshest state with zero jitter. The setInterval
fallback remains for engines without ScriptProcessorNode access.

Also reads fps.row directly in worker overlay path during playback
(bypassing React prop latency), matching the Canvas2D path behavior.

fix: per-channel FX now correctly isolates target channel only

Root cause: the worklet had two independent mute mask paths that
overwrote each other. setMuteMask (user mute/solo) and updateMainMask
(isolation exclusion) both called applyMuteMask_ on the main module,
so whichever arrived last won. When the mixer sent setMuteMask with
all-channels-active, it undid the isolation exclusion — causing ALL
channels to render through the effect chain.

Fix: the worklet now tracks both masks separately:
- userMuteMask: user mute/solo state (bit=1 means active)
- isolatedBits: channels routed to isolation slots (bit=1 means isolated)

Effective main mask = userMuteMask & ~isolatedBits, so a channel
is audible in the main output ONLY if the user hasn't muted it AND
it's not being rendered by an isolation slot.

Isolation slots also respect user mutes: slot effective mask =
channelMask & userMuteMask.

Additional safety:
- applyMuteMask_ now returns success/failure boolean
- addIsolationSlot_ aborts if muteFunc extraction fails (prevents
  unmuted isolation module from rendering all channels)
- addIsolationSlot_ aborts if applyMuteMask_ fails at runtime
- isolationError message sent to engine on failure, which cleans
  up the slot mask so main module doesn't incorrectly mute channels
- Both masks reset on play() for clean state per song
